title: Workflow graph
description: A .carina/workflows/<name>.json file (go/daemon/workflowspec.go's WorkflowSpec/WorkflowStep). Covers both the batch/BSP scheduler (default) and the streaming scheduler (execution_mode: streaming — Agent Swarm DAG orchestration design, docs/plans/2026-07-12-agent-swarm-dag-orchestration-design.md); fields marked streaming-mode-only are accepted but inert under bsp.

name.description: Workflow identifier; matches the filename by convention but is looked up by this value, not the filename.

execution_mode.description: bsp (default): batch-by-dependency-level, a step starts only once every step in the previous level finished, any single failure aborts the whole run. streaming: a step starts the instant its own dependencies resolve, failures isolate to their own dependents by default, and every field below marked streaming-mode-only becomes meaningful.

token_budget.description: Streaming-mode only. Aggregate token ceiling across every step's subagent usage for this ENTIRE run (0 = unlimited). Once spend meets the ceiling, not-yet-dispatched steps are skipped with an audited reason; steps already running are never killed.

id.description: Unique within the workflow (and within any steps a generator step later injects).

agent.description: Name of an AgentSpec to spawn for this step. Ignored (but still required by validate()) for a Remote step — the external worker's configured executor runs verbatim task text, not a named agent.

task.description: Task text handed to the spawned subagent (or, if remote/affinity, the dispatched task's prompt). Supports ${step_id} whole-output and ${step_id.field.path} typed dotted-path interpolation from completed dependencies' JSON output.

needs.description: Control-dependency step ids — this step will not start until every listed step has resolved (completed, failed, or skipped).

fail_fast.description: Streaming-mode only. A failing step normally isolates: only its transitive-only dependents are skipped, independent branches keep running. Set true to restore the old "one failure kills the whole run" behavior for a step genuinely on the critical path.

input.description: Streaming-mode only. Structured field-by-field input resolved from dependencies' JSON output and appended to the interpolated task as a labeled JSON block. Each value is either "${step_id}" (the whole prior output) or "${step_id.a.b.c}" (a typed dot-path into it, parsed as JSON — not string-substituted).

when.description: Streaming-mode only. A JSONLogic-compatible boolean expression (go/daemon/workflow_condition.go — {"var":...}, ==, !=, >, <, >=, and, or, not; sandboxed, never executable code) evaluated once this step's dependencies resolve, against their JSON-parsed outputs. A falsy or erroring result skips the step through the same isolate-propagation path an upstream failure uses; malformed expressions fail closed (skip, never silently proceed).

kind.description: Streaming-mode only. "generator" additionally parses this step's done summary for a spawn_steps envelope ({"spawn_steps":[{...new WorkflowStep objects...}],"rationale":"..."}) and injects the declared new nodes into the still-running graph — structurally cycle-safe (a new step may depend on an existing/sibling step, never the reverse), bounded by a generation-depth ceiling and the run's total step-count ceiling.

consumes_channel.description: Streaming-mode only. Swarm channel names (go/daemon/swarm_channel.go) this step subscribes to for LIVE messages from still-running sibling/upstream steps — distinct from needs/input, which only ever hand off a dependency's terminal output. The subscribed step calls the "swarm_receive" tool during its own run to pull anything published since it last checked. Publishing (the "swarm_publish" tool) needs no subscription and is available to every streaming step regardless of this field.

remote.description: Streaming-mode only. Routes this step through the cross-process dispatch/lease/report pipeline (an external worker process — e.g. apps/carina-worker — executes it, potentially on a different machine) instead of an in-process subagent. Gated by Capability::RemoteDispatch (RequiresApproval by default), a strictly stronger trust decision than the same-process SubagentSpawn gate every other step uses.

affinity.description: Streaming-mode only. Scheduling hint for cluster mode; a non-empty affinity also implies remote. The "worker_pool" key becomes a required worker capability ("worker_pool:<value>") that only a worker registered with that tag (worker.register's "pools" param, or `carina-worker --pool <tag>` / `carina worker register ... --pool <tag>`) may lease — an affinity-tagged step can never be silently picked up by an unrelated pool.
